title: Regime-Gated Experts by Strategy Context
summary: Inference from the sources: the FIA tyre rule, wet-tyre exemption, testing sessions, and competition effects imply different stop policies across dry mandatory-two-compound races, wet races, and unusual sessions.
rationale: Inference from the sources: the FIA tyre rule, wet-tyre exemption, testing sessions, and competition effects imply different stop policies across dry mandatory-two-compound races, wet races, and unusual sessions. A single global learner may be averaging incompatible decision boundaries.
implementation_hint: Train 3-4 experts on coarse regimes such as dry-race, wet/intermediate, pre-season testing, and final-stint/no-more-stop zones, then either hard-route with rules or soft-blend using a small meta-model from race_progress, compound family, weather flags, and rule-pressure features.
expected_effect: Should reduce bias from mixing fundamentally different strategic regimes and may especially help the rare wet/testing slices that distort a global model.
risk: Sample fragmentation is the main danger; only split on broad regimes with enough support and verify each expert beats the global model on its own slice.
